page.content: In the years from 2007 to 2014, the table presents data on suspected measles cases, the number of sera tested for rubella, and the corresponding Rubella IgM results. For 2007, there were 256 suspected measles cases, with 245 (95.7%) testing positive, 116 (47.3%) negative, 111 (45.3%) deemed doubtful, and 18 (7.4%) not classified. In 2008, 116 suspected cases resulted in 104 (89.6%) positive, 22 (21.2%) negative, 77 (74%), and 5 (4.8%) doubtful. The year 2009 recorded 119 suspected cases, yielding 107 (90%) positive, 33 (30.8%) negative, 69 (64.5%), and 5 (4.7%) doubtful. In 2010, there were 95 suspected cases, with 93 (97.9%) positive, 14 (15.1%) negative, 77 (82.8%), and 2 (2.1%) doubtful. The year 2011 saw 158 suspected cases, resulting in 84 (53.2%) positive, 15 (17.8%) negative, 64 (76.2%), and 5 (6%).

page.content: In 2012, there were 190 suspected cases, yielding 123 (64.7%) positive, 11 (8.9%) negative, 103 (83.8%), and 9 (7.3%) doubtful. For 2013, the data shows 380 suspected cases with 165 (43.4%) positive, 30 (18.2%) negative, 115 (69.7%), and 20 (12.1%) doubtful. Finally, in 2014, 722 suspected cases were recorded, with 488 (67.6%) testing positive, 184 (37.7%) negative, 262 (53.7%), and 42 (8.6%) doubtful. Cumulatively, the total from all years indicates 2036 suspected cases, 1409 (69.2%) positive, 425 (30.2%) negative, 878 (62.3%), and 106 (7.5%) doubtful.
